summary: Opus 3-tier catalyst sweep (chunk 19 (UCB-WEX)): scan -> deep -> skeptic

survivors_flagged.one_line: DEFINITIVE Cintas merger (3/10/26): $155 cash + 0.772 CTAS/sh (~$5.5B); shareholder vote 6/11/26 with Croatti family (2/3 vote) locked up, H2'26 close — clean dated cash+stock merger-arb, not fired.

~$85B Norfolk Southern merger live: STB accepted revised application 5/28/26, supplemental info due 7/27/26, close ~2027 — hard dated regulatory path, but the mispriced arb sits in NS (target); UNP is the acquirer.

Transformative combination with Sweetwater Royalties (Orion/Ontario Teachers'), $1.9B EV, arrangement signed Apr-16-2026, shareholder vote ~July-2026 + NASDAQ relisting; structural special situation, not fired.
Stillwater NdFeB magnet first commercial production H1-2026 + $1.6B Commerce CHIPS LOI and ~10% US-gov stake; dated milestones but more ramp/thematic than discrete arb.
Acquirer in a contested bidding war for Two Harbors (raised to $12.50 cash vs CrossCountry $12.00), Q2-2026 target close; real dated event-driven, though asymmetry muddier as the buyer.
De-SPAC with NorthStar Earth & Space (space SSA), $30M committed PIPE, BCA amended May-2026, closing Q3-2026 -> NSTR; trust-floor SPAC arb with dated close, not fired.
Transocean all-stock acquisition signed Feb-9-2026 (15.235 RIG/VAL, ~31.6% premium), DEFA14A live, close 2H-2026; clean stock-for-stock merger-arb, not fired.
Vale Base Metals (nickel/copper) carve-out/IPO accelerated to ~mid-2026 for SOTP unlock on the parent; real structural thesis but timing still 'possible', not a committed hard date.
Definitive all-stock merger into Axcelis (ACLS), close H2-2026 pending regulatory+shareholder votes — live ratio/arb spread.
Atacicept BLA PDUFA Jul-7-2026 (accel approval) PLUS ORIGIN-3 eGFR readout Q3-2026; positive P3 data already in hand — two hard imminent gates.
VFTP/VFVN split + $530M mfg-arm sale strips ~$6.9B debt off listco, targeted Q3-2026 (shareholder+creditor approvals); going-concern, founder-controlled lottery.
Calcasieu Pass arb saga; Repsol/Shell resolved, Edison settlement completes ~Q2-2026, Galp ruling still pending 2026 (BP precedent adverse) — mispriced binary tail.
Fresh Aptiv EDS spin (listed Apr-1-2026, 1:3); classic post-spin forced-seller orphan + new $250M buyback & dividend — re-rating window open now.
ITC win + ~$300M licensing largely captured (fired); structural IP-monetization continues but next hard gate (2nd ITC determination) is 2027 — too far for near-term sweep.
P3 ECLIPSE-1 hep-delta topline Q4-2026 (ECLIPSE 2/3 Q1-2027); EASL data already shown — single dated clinical binary, a couple quarters out.
Selling RUCKUS to Belden for $1.846B cash (signed Apr-29-2026, close H2-2026); large cash event vs cap drives re-rate/capital-return on RemainCo.
Dated capital return: R$4bn capital reduction (R$1.25/sh) payable Jul 14 2026 + R$1bn 2026-27 buyback + commitment to 100% FY26 payout; real but modest, parent Telefonica still controls.

Live complicated cross-border merger-of-equals with VivaAerobus (Grupo Mas Vuelos, ~69% share); 94% shareholder vote secured Mar 2026, pending Mexico CNA antitrust + HSR + Colombia ~12mo horizon; PLUS P&W GTF grounding overhang clearing w/ comp + fleet recovery late-2026.

Dated structural M&A as buyer: agreed to buy CK Hutchison's 49% of VodafoneThree for £4.3bn via share cancellation; pending UK NSI Act approval, completes H2-2026. Real but VOD is the acquirer, modest mover.

Hard dated binary: veligrotug TED BLA priority review, PDUFA Jun-30-2026 (~weeks out); launch-ready. De-risked by positive Ph3 THRIVE/THRIVE-2 + REVEAL elegrobart wins so asymmetry compressed, but a real imminent dated event.
Live activist (Carronade cooperation pact May-2026) + Strategic Review Committee, potential defense-unit spin, plus C-band spectrum cash milestones mid-2026.
Active proxy fight: BBRC (13%) urging vote against Chair at the 2026 annual meeting; activist+structural hardening with a dated meeting trigger.
Post-NCR-split restructuring: sold Digital Banking ($2.45B), Japan bank-tech sale to NTT DATA closing end-2026, ODM hardware transition - dated deleveraging/transformation milestones.
Definitive Paramount Skydance merger $31.00 cash/sh ($110B EV); shareholder vote spring-2026, close Q3-2026, $0.25/qtr ticking fee if past Sep30 2026 — live arb.
Santander acquiring at ~$75.59 ($48.75 cash + 2.0548 SAN ADS), $12.3B; vote May26 2026, close H2-2026 pending OCC/regulatory — live cash-and-stock arb.

verified.catalyst: UniFirst is being acquired by Cintas under a definitive 3/10/26 agreement for $155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/share (~$294/sh, ~$5.5B), with the shareholder vote 6/11/26 and Croatti-family 2/3 lock-up making approval near-certain.
verified.dated_milestone: Special shareholder vote 6/11/26 (next); HSR waiting period currently expires 6/11/26 11:59pm ET after Cintas pull-and-refile (5/8 withdraw, 5/12 refile), extendable by a second request; H2'26 expected close.
verified.instrument: stock (merger-arb long UNF, optionally hedged short 0.7720 CTAS to isolate the ~$24/sh cash+spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Cintas/CTAS), G2 (concrete definitive merger commitment, $155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS), G3 (specific unpriced spread: deal value ~$293.84 vs UNF $269.92 = ~8.9% gross)
verified.edge: M — clean dated cash+stock terms with 2/3 vote locked up (vote risk minimal), but the ~8.9%/$24 spread is compensation for real FTC antitrust risk (#1+#2 uniform rental, pull-and-refile signals hard look, second request widely expected, litigation possible), so this is the riskier end of arb, not free money.
verified.primary_source: UniFirst DEFM14A / Cintas 424B3 (SEC, filed ~5/2026; merger agreement dated 3/10/26; vote 6/11/26; HSR refile 5/12/26, waiting period to 6/11/26) — sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0000717954/000114036126020380; quotes UNF $269.92 / CTAS $179.85 via FMP 6/6/26.
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm every load-bearing fact: definitive Cintas/UniFirst merger signed 3/10/26 (BusinessWire 3/10/26; announced 3/11), consideration $155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/share verbatim in DEFM14A/S-4 (~$5.5B), special meeting 6/11/26, Croatti-affiliated holders (~2/3 voting power) under a voting/support agreement. HSR: filed 4/8/26, withdrawn effective 5/8/26, refiled 5/12/26, clock to 6/11/26 11:59pm (MLex/S-4) — second request widely expected. Live & forward: UNF $269.92 vs consideration ~$293.84 (0.7720 x CTAS $179.85 + $155) = ~$23.9/sh, ~8.9% gross spread, unclosed. Idiosyncratic, dated, non-macro merger-arb; the wide spread reflects genuine antitrust overhang (two largest US uniform-rental players), which correctly caps conviction at 7 not 8. Near-term watch 6/11/26 vote + HSR/second-request outcome.
verified.analysis: Confirmed real, dated, forward, idiosyncratic merger-arb: definitive 3/10/26 Cintas/UNF agreement, $155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS (~$294/sh), shareholder vote 6/11/26 with Croatti family (~2/3 of votes) locked up; UNF trades $269.92 vs ~$294 consideration, an ~8.9% gross spread that is live/unpriced, so not fired. The wide spread reflects genuine antitrust overhang — the two largest uniform-rental players combining, with Cintas's HSR pull-and-refile (withdrew 5/8, refiled 5/12, clock to 6/11) and a widely expected FTC second request plus possible litigation — which caps conviction at score 7 rather than the triage's 8. Catalyst clears all three Bloom gates and the dated/forward/non-macro tests; near-term watch is the 6/11/26 vote and whether a second request is issued versus an HSR-clears outcome.

verified.catalyst: UNP is the acquirer in the ~$85B Norfolk Southern transcontinental merger now in STB review; the dated arb/regulatory path is real but the mispriced spread sits in NS (target), not in UNP.
verified.dated_milestone: STB accepted revised application 5/28/26 and held proceedings in abeyance; supplemental info (terminals/car supply, market shares, downstream mergers) due 7/27/26; decision ~2027, close mid-2027. Next date: 2026-07-27.
verified.instrument: equity (none recommended on the acquirer leg)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 partial (named counterparty NS); G2/G3 FAIL on the UNP leg — no concrete commitment or unpriced figure accrues to the acquirer (the deal spread is NS-side)
verified.edge: L — wrong leg: arb spread lives in NS; STB approval is a binary 2027 event UNP bears as risk (integration/dilution), not a clean value-crystallizing catalyst, and UNP has already re-rated to ~$272 near its 52wk high.
verified.primary_source: STB Press Release PR-26-13 "STB Accepts UP-NS Merger Application for Consideration; Requires Supplemental Information and Holds Proceedings in Abeyance" (stb.gov, dated 2026-05-28); corroborated by Bloomberg (2026-05-28) and FMP batch-quote (UNP $272.32, NSC $313.45, as of 2026-06-05 close).
verified.analysis: The dated regulatory path is verified via primary STB source (PR-26-13, 5/28/26): revised application accepted, proceedings in abeyance, supplemental information due 7/27/26, with a Board decision expected ~2027 and close mid-2027. But the actionable mispricing sits in NS (the target, where the cash+stock deal spread compresses), not in UNP, which as acquirer carries regulatory/integration/dilution risk rather than a re-rating spread — and UNP has already moved up (+3.2% today to $272.32, near the $279.70 52wk high, well above its $240 200dma). This is a real merger but the wrong leg: no specific currently-unpriced figure accrues to the acquirer, so it fails strict gating for a UNP forward catalyst.

verified.catalyst: UROY combines with Sweetwater Royalties (sellers: Orion Resource Partners + Ontario Teachers') via CBCA plan of arrangement into a new US-domiciled NASDAQ-relisted royalty platform, pending a ~July-2026 shareholder vote.
verified.dated_milestone: Special shareholder meeting (66 2/3% approval required) on or about July 2026, then court/regulatory approval + NASDAQ relisting of New URC, closing thereafter; next hard date = July-2026 vote.
verified.instrument: equity (continuing-acquirer common; no listed UROY options arb leg)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Orion/Ontario Teachers' + Sweetwater named, signed Apr-16-2026) and G2 (binding $1.9B-EV arrangement, $330M cash + $813M shares at deemed $3.64, UEC $40M backstop closed May-1-2026 at $3.64, 66 2/3% vote)
verified.edge: L — lane is mislabeled: UROY is the SURVIVING ACQUIRER (existing holders end ~41% of NewCo), so there is NO cash-takeout spread to converge; the deemed $3.64 is seller consideration, not a price floor for UROY holders, and the stock floats with deal/dilution/uranium-NAV risk (down 13.4% to $3.09 on 2026-06-06, below the $3.64 deemed level). No unpriced arb spread + commodity/equity risk = low edge.
verified.primary_source: UROY/Orion/Ontario Teachers' joint press release "Create a Leading Royalty Platform" (Apr-16-2026, via Nasdaq/Newswire/StockTitan); UROY private-placement closing PR (May-1-2026); FMP quote $3.09, -13.4% (2026-06-06).
verified.analysis: The combination is real, named, dated and forward (vote ~July-2026, NASDAQ relisting), satisfying Bloom G1+G2, but it is a transformative reverse-style combination, not a UROY buyout — UROY shareholders roll into a larger, more-levered ($625M debt) NewCo rather than being cashed out, so the merger_arb thesis of a converging spread does not apply. With the share price already below the deemed $3.64 and falling hard today, the setup carries genuine deal-approval, dilution and uranium-price risk on the equity rather than a low-risk arb. Legitimate structural special-situation/re-rating optionality keeps it on WATCH, but it does not clear the strict gate as a fired, unpriced merger-arb catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Stillwater NdFeB magnet Phase 1a production and the $1.6B Commerce/CHIPS package are both already announced; only a multi-quarter capacity ramp and generic "begin fulfilling orders" guidance remain forward.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 1a ramp to 600 mtpa run-rate by end of Q4-2026 (next: 1,200 mtpa total Stillwater capacity in Q1-2027); generic "begin fulfilling customer orders" in Q2-2026 with no named customer.
verified.instrument: equity
verified.bloom_gates: none (no G1 named offtake counterparty; no G2 binding offtake commitment with dollar value; no G3 specific currently-unpriced figure — only ramp-capacity guidance)
verified.edge: L — both named catalysts are fired and re-rated; stock "sold the news" -11% on 2026-06-05 (from $27.10) on the June 3 CHIPS finalization, confirming the package was already priced; residual is thematic rare-earth ramp, not discrete arb.
verified.primary_source: USA Rare Earth GlobeNewswire PR, Stillwater Phase 1a commissioning (2026-03-26) and CHIPS definitive-agreement finalization (2026-06-03); MINING.COM 2026-06-03; sell-the-news -11% on 2026-06-05 (StockTitan/Tickeron).
verified.analysis: Both flagged catalysts are confirmed but already occurred: Stillwater Phase 1a was commissioned 2026-03-26 and the $1.6B CHIPS definitive agreements (with ~10% gov equity via 16.1M shares + warrant at $17.17) were finalized 2026-06-03, after which USAR fell ~11% in a textbook sell-the-news, evidencing the package was pre-positioned. There is no named offtake counterparty, no binding offtake with a dollar value, and no specific unpriced figure — the only forward items are multi-quarter capacity-ramp guidance (600 mtpa Q4-2026, 1,200 mtpa Q1-2027) and generic "begin fulfilling orders" in Q2-2026. This is a thematic/macro mine-to-magnet ramp bet rather than a discrete, dated, currently-unpriced arb, so it fails the strict gate.

verified.catalyst: UWMC is the higher bidder ($12.50 cash or 2.3328 UWMC shares, no cap/proration) in a contested takeover of Two Harbors (TWO) against CrossCountry's board-recommended $12.00 cash, decided at TWO's twice-adjourned special stockholder vote.
verified.dated_milestone: Two Harbors special stockholder meeting reconvenes June 11, 2026, 10:00 a.m. ET (adjourned from May 19 -> May 28 -> June 11); ISS recommended AGAINST the CCM deal citing UWMC's bid.
verified.instrument: equity
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Two Harbors/CrossCountry) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: $12.50 vs $12.00 bid, June 11 vote); G2 not a clean fit.
verified.edge: L — UWMC is the ACQUIRER, not the target, so there is no clean fixed-cash arb spread; the directional payoff is ambiguous (winning a contested REIT bid with stock near 52-wk lows is potentially dilutive, losing removes an overhang). TWO's own board flags 25-30% of cash-election laggards would default into UWMC stock worth ~$7.23, undercutting the $12.50 headline.
verified.primary_source: Mortgage Professional, "UWMC raises pressure on Two Harbors ahead of crucial June 11 vote" (2026-06-04/05) and UWMC/TWO open letters; UWMC quote $2.59 via FMP (2026-06-05 close).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, named, and dated (June 11 TWO vote), and the triage facts check out — UWMC raised to $12.50 May 11, HSR early-terminated May 21, 41/53 regulatory approvals obtained, ISS backs UWMC's premium. But UWMC sits on the buyer side: at $2.59 (near 52-wk low $2.57, off a $7.14 high), the equity-leg currency is its own depressed stock, and a win means deploying cash/issuing stock to absorb a mortgage REIT — a muddy, arguably negative-skew outcome rather than the long-spread asymmetry merger_arb usually rewards. Passes the structural gates (dated/forward/specific) so not NONE, but the buyer-side ambiguity caps it at a low-conviction WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Viking Acquisition Corp. I (VACI) de-SPAC merger with NorthStar Earth & Space (space situational-awareness/SSA) backed by a $30M fully-committed PIPE anchored by Cartesian Capital Group, redomiciling to Canada and relisting as NSTR.
verified.dated_milestone: Definitive BCA dated Apr-16-2026, amended ~May-18-2026; F-4 to be filed then definitive proxy + Viking shareholder vote; expected close Q3-2026; outside/termination date Jan-31-2027.
verified.instrument: VACI common (trust-floor SPAC arb; redemption right ~$10 NAV floor, free NSTR optionality above)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 + G2 (named counterparty NorthStar/Cartesian Capital + concrete $30M committed PIPE & dated definitive BCA). G3 only weakly: edge is a redemption-protected NAV floor, not a quantified discount-to-trust.
verified.edge: M — trust floor (~$10 NAV) caps downside and SSA optionality is unpriced, but VACI trades $10.30 (slightly ABOVE trust), so there is no discount-to-trust to harvest; SPAC-arb returns are thin and NSTR upside is speculative pre-vote.
verified.primary_source: SEC 8-K/425 Viking Acquisition Corp. I (CIK 0002080023), filed Apr-17-2026; NorthStar GlobeNewswire PR Apr-17-2026; Boardroom Alpha "Daily SPAC: VACI-NorthStar" May-18-2026; FMP quote VACI $10.30 (Jun-06-2026).
verified.kill_fact: Primary SEC filing confirms: "On April 16, 2026, Viking Acquisition Corp. I [a Cayman exempted company] entered into a Business Combination Agreement with NorthStar Earth and Space Inc." announced Apr-17; $30M fully-committed PIPE anchored by Cartesian Capital, $300M pre-money, ~30M closing + up to 10M earnout shares, relists as NSTR on NYSE. Amendment No. 1 (dated May 15, 2026 — NOT ~May-18) revised redemption timing (public-share redemption now occurs prior to the continuation from the Cayman Islands to Canada and prior to Closing), closing-step structure (share/warrant conversions, equity exchanges in the Amalgamation) and intended U.S./Canadian tax treatment; Outside Date = Jan-31-2027, close expected Q3-2026. Live FMP quote: VACI $10.30, 52wk $9.86-$10.49, vol ~292K, NYSE — confirms it hugs trust near NAV (no entry discount), not fired/not re-rated, F-4/proxy + Viking vote still pending. Two corrections: (1) amendment is May-15 not ~May-18; (2) "redomiciling to Canada" is precisely a continuation FROM the Cayman Islands TO Canada (VACI is a Cayman SPAC; NorthStar is already a Montreal/Canadian company). Triage relabel from "Voyager" to Viking is correct.
verified.analysis: Verified real and dated: definitive BCA Apr-16-2026 (announced Apr-17), $30M fully-committed PIPE anchored by Cartesian Capital, $300M pre-money, rebrands to NSTR on NYSE, expected close Q3-2026 with a Jan-31-2027 outside date; a ~May-18-2026 amendment revised redemption timing/closing steps and tax treatment (Boardroom Alpha, SEC 425). Not fired and not re-rated — VACI hugs trust at $10.30 (52wk $9.86–$10.49) ahead of the F-4/proxy and Viking shareholder vote, so the redemption right gives a NAV-floor arb with free SSA optionality but no cheap entry discount. Triage labels corrected: the counterparty is Viking Acquisition Corp. I, not "Voyager" (Voyager Acq. is a separate deal with Veraxa Biotech), and the BCA was originated Apr-2026 then amended May-2026.

verified.catalyst: All-stock acquisition of Valaris by Transocean at a fixed 15.235 RIG/VAL ratio (definitive BCA signed Feb-9-2026), now gated on DOJ antitrust clearance with a ~2.4% gross spread still open to deal terms.
verified.dated_milestone: DOJ Second Request issued May-4-2026 extends HSR waiting period until 30 days after substantial compliance; next hard gate = substantial-compliance/clearance resolution (mid-to-late 2026), with target close still 2H-2026. (Valaris Court Meeting / Transocean EGM dates pending in DEFA14A — not extractable in budget.)
verified.instrument: VAL common (long) vs RIG (short) at 15.235x — stock-for-stock merger-arb; or long VAL outright for deal-completion exposure.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Transocean), G2 (concrete commitment: signed definitive Business Combination Agreement, fixed exchange ratio + ~9% RIG and ~18% VAL holder support agreements); G3 partial (currently-unpriced ~2.4% gross arb spread + post-Second-Request completion-probability discount).
verified.edge: M — clean locked-ratio stock-for-stock with ~27% combined committed shareholder support, but the May-4-2026 DOJ Second Request is a live two-sided risk: it materially extends timing and raises deal-break tail in a top-2 offshore-driller combination (~73 rigs), and the all-stock ratio carries RIG-beta. Spread is thin (~2.4%) for the residual risk, capping conviction.
verified.primary_source: SEC DEFA14A / Globe & Mail "Valaris–Transocean Merger Faces Extended DOJ Antitrust Review" (Second Request, May-4-2026); BCA announced Feb-9-2026; FMP batch-quote RIG $5.95 / VAL $88.54 (2026-06-06).
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts confirmed vs primary sources. SEC/press confirm definitive all-stock BCA signed Feb-9-2026 at fixed 15.235 RIG/VAL ratio (Transocean acquires Valaris; VAL holders get 15.235 RIG each). Primary RIG 8-K confirms DOJ Second Request received May-4-2026, extending HSR period (initial HSR filed Mar-2-2026), targeted 2H-2026 close — deal NOT fired, live and forward. Live FMP quotes: VAL $88.54 (-4.70%), RIG $5.95 (-4.80%); deal value 15.235x$5.95=$90.65 => gross spread ~2.38% (LINE's ~2.4% accurate), both names down ~4.7% today as stated. Genuine completion/timing antitrust-gated arb, not a macro oil bet. Two-sided antitrust risk on top-2 offshore-driller consolidation + thin residual spread justify gated 7. Only minor unconfirmed nuance: 'spread widened today' (prior-day spread not independently checked) and the noted gap (meeting dates/End Date) — neither material. Score 7 sustained.
verified.analysis: Confirmed real, dated, and forward: definitive all-stock BCA signed Feb-9-2026 at a fixed 15.235 RIG/VAL ratio, with the deal NOT fired — VAL trades at ~$88.54 vs ~$90.65 deal value (15.235 x RIG $5.95), a ~2.4% gross spread that widened today as both names fell ~4.7%. The dominant live catalyst is the DOJ Second Request issued May-4-2026, which extends the HSR waiting period and is the binding gate to a still-targeted 2H-2026 close; this is genuine completion/timing-probability arb, not a macro oil bet. Gated ACTIVE at 7 rather than higher because the antitrust Second Request on a top-2 offshore-driller consolidation is a meaningful two-sided risk and the residual spread is thin; exact shareholder-meeting dates and the agreement End Date could not be pinned within the lookup budget (noted gap).

verified.catalyst: Vale Base Metals (nickel/copper) carve-out IPO accelerated toward mid-2026 "IPO-ready," a potential SOTP unlock (~$40B unit) on the VALE parent.
verified.dated_milestone: CEO targets unit "IPO-ready" by ~mid-2026 (no committed listing date); next checkpoint is the H1/Q2 2026 results/strategy update where Vale's board could authorize a process.
verified.instrument: equity (VALE ADR)
verified.bloom_gates: None fully met. G1: no listing counterparty/underwriter named for the IPO itself (Manara 10% stake and Thompson Belt consortium are separate, already-closed sub-deals, not the carve-out event). G2: FAILS — Vale formally told CVM there are "no formal studies or management decisions" on any offering. G3: ~$40B unit valuation is an analyst/industry estimate, not a company-committed unpriced figure.
verified.edge: M — real structural SOTP thesis with credible acceleration (CEO on record, 2027→mid-2026), but the parent explicitly retains the decision and has disclaimed any commitment, so timing is "possible," not investable on a hard date.
verified.primary_source: Vale S.A. Form 6-K filed 2026-03-04 (CVM clarification: "no formal studies or management decisions" on an IPO); MINING.COM 2026-03-03 (CEO Usmar: IPO-ready by ~midyear); TipRanks/Globe and Mail 2026-03 (regulator-query clarification).
verified.analysis: The thesis is genuine: CEO Shaun Usmar publicly accelerated Base Metals "IPO-ready" status from 2027 to ~mid-2026 (MINING.COM, Mar 3 2026), and the unit is plausibly worth ~$40B, a real SOTP catalyst for the parent. But the gate fails on commitment/date: in a March 4 2026 6-K responding to a CVM query, Vale stressed there are currently no formal studies or management decisions regarding any offering, and management itself frames "IPO-ready" as operational readiness ("present choice to our owners"), not a listing decision. No named IPO counterparty, no committed date, and the headline valuation is an estimate — so this is a watch-the-board-authorization item, not a fired or hard-dated forward catalyst.

verified.catalyst: All-stock merger into Axcelis (ACLS) at a fixed 0.3575 ACLS-per-VECO ratio, with only China SAMR antitrust approval left before an expected H2-2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: China SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) clearance / deal close — expected H2-2026; next hard gate is SAMR sign-off (no fixed date, both parties reaffirmed H2-2026 as of the Feb 6, 2026 vote).
verified.instrument: equity (VECO common); conventional arb = long VECO / short 0.3575 ACLS, but spread is inverted
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Axcelis) + G2 (concrete definitive merger agreement, fixed 0.3575 exchange ratio)
verified.edge: L — the convergence edge is inverted and the main de-risking event already fired
verified.primary_source: PRNewswire "Axcelis Stockholders Approve Merger with Veeco" + Yahoo/Finance "Axcelis-Veeco $4.4bn merger receives shareholder approval" (Feb 6, 2026); FMP batch-quote VECO $58.45 / ACLS $147.16 (Jun 6, 2026 close)
verified.analysis: The deal is real, dated, and specific (definitive all-stock merger, 0.3575 ACLS/VECO, both shareholder votes passed Feb 6 2026, only China SAMR left, close H2-2026), but the headline arb catalyst has largely FIRED: votes are done and a single foreign regulator remains. Critically, at the Jun 6 2026 close ACLS $147.16 × 0.3575 = $52.61 implied vs VECO $58.45, so the spread is NEGATIVE ~-10% — VECO trades 11% ABOVE deal value, meaning a standard long-VECO arb loses on convergence and the market is implicitly pricing a topping bid or deal-break re-rate. With the conventional edge inverted and only binary China-regulatory risk left, this is a WATCH (track SAMR headlines), not an actionable ACTIVE long-arb.

verified.catalyst: Two hard imminent FDA gates for atacicept in IgA nephropathy: PDUFA accelerated-approval decision and the pulled-forward ORIGIN-3 eGFR readout that underpins full approval.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA accelerated-approval action date Jul-7-2026; ORIGIN-3 eGFR readout Q3-2026 (then sBLA Q4-2026)
verified.instrument: equity (binary biotech catalyst; long-call overlay viable into Jul-7)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty = FDA, statutory PDUFA date) + G2 (concrete FDA-aligned committed milestone: ORIGIN-3 eGFR Q3-2026 + sBLA Q4-2026)
verified.edge: M — both gates are real, dated, FDA-bound and de-risked by positive pivotal ORIGIN data (46% proteinuria reduction, NEJM); but the Jun-2 acceleration news is already public so partially priced, capping asymmetry. Stock at $32 vs $56 52w-high and below 50/200d MAs implies the binary approval/readout outcomes are not yet re-rated in.
verified.primary_source: Vera Therapeutics IR / StockTitan PR "Alignment with FDA on Earlier ORIGIN Phase 3 Analysis" (Jun-2-2026) + FDA priority-review/PDUFA Jul-7-2026 grant

verified.analysis: Triage thesis verified: VSAT entered a May-7-2026 cooperation agreement with Carronade Capital (G1) that seated two activist-backed directors (Shekar Ayyar, Jinhy Yoon) onto a Strategic Review Committee now explicitly weighing separation of the government/defense (DAT) unit from commercial, with management guiding an FY2027-H1 outlook update (G2) - a genuine, dated, forward, non-macro activist-structural catalyst that has NOT fired (no separation decided as of June 2026). The C-band leg is weaker for gating because the $420M Ligado lump-sum is already received and sits in net debt, so it is priced/fired rather than a forward G3 figure. ACTIVE at 7 on a clean G1+G2 pairing; next observable checkpoint is FQ1 FY2027 earnings (~early-Aug 2026) for review progress, with the Carronade standstill anchored to the 2027 annual meeting.

verified.catalyst: BBRC (13%, second-largest holder) is running an active proxy contest urging shareholders to vote against Chair Donna James and director Mariam Naficy at the VSCO/VSXY 2026 annual meeting.
verified.dated_milestone: 2026 Annual Meeting vote — June 11, 2026 (5 days forward).
verified.instrument: stock (NYSE: VSXY, formerly VSCO; ticker changed June 2, 2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: BBRC International PTE vs. VSCO board; definitive proxy filed). G2 fails (board is contesting, not committing). G3 fails (BBRC's $1.2B buyback / $155.9M Adore Me impairment figures are backward-looking grievances, already disclosed/priced).
verified.edge: L — all three proxy advisors (ISS, Glass Lewis, Egan-Jones) recommended FOR management on June 3, making an activist defeat on June 11 the base case; stock already re-rated from ~$50 (50dMA) to ~$74.60 near year-high $81.25 (+17% on the fight per Simply Wall St), so optionality is largely priced; target Naficy already withdrew.
verified.primary_source: StockTitan / GlobeNewswire: "All Three Independent Proxy Advisors Recommend Victoria's Secret Shareholders Vote FOR Company Nominees" (2026-06-03); BBRC DFAN14A / Business Wire "BBRC Calls on Stockholders to Vote Against the Directors" (2026-05-04); meeting date June 11, 2026.
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, specific, dated and forward: a named 13% activist (BBRC) with a filed definitive proxy targeting the Chair at a June 11, 2026 vote (G1 satisfied), so it clears the hard gates. But the unpriced asymmetry is weak — all three independent proxy advisors backed the incumbent board on June 3, making activist defeat the likely outcome, and the shares have already run hard (50dMA ~$50 to ~$74.60), so a board win on June 11 is more "uncertainty removed" than a re-rating driver. With no board concession (G2) or specific unpriced figure (G3) and a depleted edge, this is a WATCH/event-risk item rather than an ACTIVE long; triage's ACTIVE/6 overstates given the advisor sweep and prior re-rate.

verified.catalyst: Sale of the Japan bank-technology unit to NTT DATA is the only still-forward divestiture in NCR Voyix's deleveraging transformation, but terms are undisclosed and the unit is small/non-core.
verified.dated_milestone: NTT DATA Japan bank-tech sale expected to close by end of 2026 (announced 2026-03-17); no firm date set.
verified.instrument: equity
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty NTT DATA) only; fails G3 (terms undisclosed, no specific unpriced figure) and the broader thesis fails FORWARD (ODM transition + Digital Banking divestiture + $1.84B debt cut + $300M buyback already fired).
verified.edge: L — the deleveraging/transformation narrative is in-market and largely fired: ODM hardware transition completed end-Q1 2026, Digital Banking sold 2024, net leverage already 2.1x, $300M buyback authorized Feb 2026. Japan sale is immaterial and termless.
verified.primary_source: NCR Voyix Q1 2026 results (Business Wire, 2026-05-07) confirming ODM transition completed end-Q1; Japan/NTT DATA sale announcement (Business Wire, 2026-03-17, close by end-2026, terms undisclosed); FMP quote 2026-06-06 ($6.91, 52wk low $6.02).
verified.analysis: The triage's "dated deleveraging/transformation milestones" are mostly already-fired (ODM hardware transition completed end of Q1 2026 per the 2026-05-07 Q1 report; the $2.45B Digital Banking sale and $1.84B debt reduction were 2024 events) or already-guided (FY2026 adjusted FCF $190-220M, net leverage 2.1x, $300M repurchase authorization). The only genuinely forward item — the Japan bank-tech sale to NTT DATA (named counterparty, G1) closing end-2026 — has undisclosed terms (fails G3, no unpriced figure) and is a small non-core unit unlikely to re-rate a $950M-cap equity sitting near its 52-week low ($6.91 vs $6.02 low). Net: no specific, currently-unpriced, dated forward catalyst; a transformation-execution watch item rather than an actionable ACTIVE catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Definitive Paramount Skydance acquisition of WBD at $31.00/share cash (~$110B EV) with a live ~18% gross arb spread gated on US DOJ + EU antitrust clearance and a fresh state-AG block-suit threat.
verified.dated_milestone: EU preliminary antitrust decision due July 7, 2026; expected deal close Q3-2026; ticking fee $0.25/qtr to WBD holders if not closed past Dec 31, 2026 (next: EU July 7).
verified.instrument: stock (WBD common, merger-arb long to $31 cash; or LEAPS calls expiring post-Q3-2026)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Paramount Skydance), G2 (binding $31/sh cash merger agreement), G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~$4.76/$26.24 gross spread = ~18% to fixed $31 cash price)
verified.edge: M - spread is real and wide (~18% gross, ~22% annualized to a Q3 close) but capped upside vs binary downside to ~$12.54 unaffected; dominant driver is regulatory/litigation, and the June 5 state-AG lawsuit news materially raises break risk.
verified.primary_source: Reuters "US states preparing lawsuit to block Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros" (2026-06-05); Reuters "Paramount seeks EU approval; decision due July 7" (2026-06-02); WBD press release shareholder approval (2026-04-23)
verified.kill_fact: Primary sources confirm the core: Paramount Skydance's definitive $31.00/sh all-cash acquisition of WBD (~$110.9B, Paramount press release + SEC 8-Ks), shareholder vote FIRED Apr 23 2026, and a live wide spread — FMP quote shows WBD $26.24, -2.81% on Jun 5 (gross spread to $31 = ~18.1%), exactly matching the LINE. The catalyst is genuinely fresh and forward (close NOT fired): Reuters (Jun 5 13:29 ET) and NY Post (Jun 5 13:54) report a group of up to ~10 states (CA-led, AG Bonta) preparing an antitrust suit to block the deal, possibly filed before end-June; Motley Fool ties the -2.81% drop directly to this. EU decision due Jul 7 (Reuters, Jun 2); DOJ reportedly leaning approve (Semafor). CORRECTIONS: (1) the Semafor DOJ report is dated ~May 26 (Tue), not May 27, and Reuters could not independently verify it. (2) The LINE's headline 'correction' overstates certainty — the ticking-fee outside date is genuinely AMBIGUOUS in the filings: the deal-enhancement language frames $0.25/qtr (~$650M) accruing for quarters unclosed past Dec 31 2026, while the merger-agreement measurement clause references Sep 30 2026; so 'Dec 31 not Sep 30' is not cleanly correct. The $0.25/qtr figure IS correct. None of these break the merger-arb thesis: tradeable (liquid common ~49M ADV, deep options), real edge in an unpriced spread reflecting live EU + multi-state antitrust risk, not a story/priced-in name.
verified.analysis: The $31 cash deal is definitive and shareholder-approved (vote FIRED Apr 23 2026), but the close is NOT fired: WBD trades $26.24 (Jun 5), a wide ~18% gross spread that reflects live antitrust risk, with EU's preliminary decision due Jul 7 and US DOJ reportedly leaning approve (Semafor May 27). The catalyst re-energized on Jun 5 when Reuters/NYP reported a group of US states preparing a lawsuit to block the deal, knocking WBD -2.8% and widening the arb. One correction to the triage flag: the ticking fee accrues for quarters unclosed past Dec 31 2026 (per filings/Reuters), not Sep 30; the $0.25/qtr figure is correct.

verified.catalyst: Banco Santander's definitive $12.3B cash-and-stock acquisition of Webster Financial ($48.75 cash + 2.0548 SAN ADS, ~$75.59 headline) now awaits Fed/OCC/ECB regulatory approval to close, with a ~2% gross spread still open at the live SAN-floated deal value of ~$73.72 vs WBS $72.27.
verified.dated_milestone: Regulatory approvals (Fed/OCC/ECB) → close H2-2026; per proxy earliest ~6 weeks post-vote (~early July 2026). Next: deal close. Shareholder vote already FIRED May 26, 2026 (passed 115.8M-1.3M).
verified.instrument: WBS common (cash-and-stock arb long WBS; stock leg carries SAN equity/FX beta — hedge via short 2.0548 SAN per share to isolate spread)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Banco Santander) + G2 (concrete definitive merger terms: $48.75 + 2.0548 SAN ADS, $12.3B)
verified.edge: L — the flagged shareholder-vote leg already fired (May 26); remaining catalyst is a soft-dated H2-2026 regulatory close with only ~2% gross spread and an unhedged SAN equity/FX leg (SAN -2.6% today), so the "$75.59" headline overstates realizable value vs the live ~$73.72.
verified.primary_source: Webster 8-K shareholder-vote result (passed May 26 2026) + DEFM14A merger proxy; deal terms per Santander press release Feb 3 2026 and InsideArbitrage Feb 2026; live WBS $72.27 / SAN $12.15 quotes 2026-06-06.
verified.analysis: The Santander/Webster deal is real and definitive (terms verified: $48.75 cash + 2.0548 SAN ADS, $12.3B, Feb 3 2026 agreement), but the primary dated milestone the triage flagged — the May 26 2026 shareholder vote — has already fired and passed overwhelmingly, making that framing stale. What remains is a genuine forward leg: Fed/OCC/ECB regulatory approval and close in H2-2026, with a ~2% gross spread still open, so it is not fully re-rated. It clears as a real merger-arb (G1+G2) but the edge is low: the spread is thin, the close date is soft ("H2-2026"), and the value floats with SAN (down 2.6% today), so the headline $75.59 is misleading versus the live ~$73.72 — WATCH, not ACTIVE.
